description: Figure 4 presents total electron density distribution contours (left column, a, c, e, g, i) and electron density difference distribution contours (right column, b, d, f, h, j) for the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl alloy system computed via DFT. The five compositions shown are FeAl-(011) (a, b), Fe_{0.75}Mn_{0.25}Al-(011) (c, d), Fe_{0.5}Mn_{0.5}Al-(110) (e, f), Fe_{0.25}Mn_{0.75}Al-(110) (g, h), and MnAl-(110) (i, j), with Al in purple, Fe in dark blue, and Mn in green spheres. The total density maps reveal high electron density in the core regions and elongated contours along Al-Fe, Al-Mn, and Fe-Mn bond axes, indicating covalent interactions. The difference maps show electron accumulation (red/yellow) and depletion (blue) regions, revealing delocalized electrons between atoms alongside localized electrons around Mn, Fe, and Al. These features confirm a combination of covalent and metallic bonding, which the authors associate with high melting points and high hardness.
